heading: Micro-purchase threshold
label: 3573 Micro-purchase threshold
text: The micro-purchase threshold for the Department of Defense is $10,000.
notes: Prior ProvisionsPrior sections 3573 and 3574 were repealed by , title VII, § 701, , , 2955, effective .Pub. L. 96–513, title II, § 21194 Stat. 2885Dec. 12, 1980Sept. 15, 1981Section 3573, , , specified the date of rank of an officer whose regular grade is brigadier general and the date of rank of an officer whose regular grade is major general and provided that the names of general officers of the Regular Army be carried on a seniority list in the order of seniority in both regular grade and date of rank. See .act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 204section 741 of this titleSection 3574, acts , ; , , §§ 1(86), 33(a)(24), , 1565, provided for determination of rank of commissioned officers of the same grade in the Regular Army who are on the same promotion list, rank of commissioned officers of the same grade in the Regular Army who are not on the same promotion list or not on a promotion list, and rank among graduates of each class at the United States Military, Naval, or Air Force Academies who, upon graduation, are appointed to the Regular Army. See .Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 205Pub. L. 85–86172 Stat. 1481section 741 of this titleSept. 2, 1958A prior section 3575 was renumbered .section 7225 of this titlePrior sections 3576 and 3578 were repealed by , , .Pub. L. 90–235, § 5(a)(2)81 Stat. 761Jan. 2, 1968Section 3576, , , provided for command when different commands of the Army and Marine Corps joined or served together. See .act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 205section 747 of this titleSection 3578, , , provided for command when two or more commissioned officers of the Army in the same grade were on duty at the same place. See .act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 205section 749 of this titleA prior section 3579 was renumbered .section 7229 of this titleA prior section 3580, , , provided that the Secretary of the Army prescribe the military authority that commissioned officers of the Women’s Army Corps may exercise, prior to repeal by , , .act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 206Pub. L. 95–485, title VIII, § 820(f)92 Stat. 1627Oct. 20, 1978A prior section 3581 was renumbered .section 7231 of this titleA prior section 3582, , , provided that a retired officer has no right to command except when on active duty, prior to repeal by , title VII, § 701, , , 2955, effective . See .act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 206Pub. L. 96–513, title II, § 21194 Stat. 2885section 750 of this titleDec. 12, 1980Sept. 15, 1981A prior section 3583 was renumbered .section 7233 of this titlePrior sections 3611 and 3612 were repealed by , , .Pub. L. 90–235, § 8(2)81 Stat. 764Jan. 2, 1968Section 3611, , , provided that the President could prescribe the uniform of the Army.act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 206Section 3612, , , provided for disposition of uniforms of enlisted members of Army who were discharged and for disposition of uniforms of and issuance of civilian clothing to enlisted members of Army who were discharged otherwise than honorably.act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 206A prior section 3631, , , prohibited officers of the Quartermaster Corps of the Army and officers performing duties of officers of that branch from dealing in quartermaster supplies, prior to repeal by , , .act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 207Pub. L. 90–235, § 7(b)(1)81 Stat. 763Jan. 2, 1968Prior sections 3632 and 3633 were repealed by , §§ 14c(6), (7), 15, , , 502, effective .Pub. L. 87–64976 Stat. 501Sept. 7, 1962Nov. 1, 1962Section 3632, , , provided for forfeiture of pay during absence from duty due to disease from intemperate use of alcohol or drugs. See , Pay and Allowances of the Uniformed Services.act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 207section 802 of Title 37Section 3633, , , provided for forfeiture of pay of commissioned officers when dropped from rolls. See .act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 207section 803 of Title 37A prior section 3634, , ; , , , generally prohibited Army band from being paid for performance outside Army post, prior to repeal by , , . L. 90–235, § 6(a)(7)81 Stat. 762Jan. 2, 1968A prior section 3636, , , provided that pay and allowances do not accrue to an enlisted member of Army who is in confinement under sentence of dishonorable discharge, while execution of sentence to discharge is suspended, prior to repeal by , §§ 14c(8), 15, , , 502, effective . See .act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 208Pub. L. 87–64976 Stat. 501section 858b of this titleSept. 7, 1962Nov. 1, 1962A prior section 3637, , , provided that an enlisted member of the Army who deserted forfeited all right to a pension, prior to repeal by , , .act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 208Pub. L. 90–235, § 7(b)(1)81 Stat. 763Jan. 2, 1968A prior section 3638, , , required enlisted members to make up time lost, prior to repeal by , , . See .act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 208Pub. L. 85–861, § 36B(8)72 Stat. 1570section 972(a) of this titleSept. 2, 1958A prior section 3639 was renumbered .section 7239 of this title

Amendments2021— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 116–283section 2338 of this title2018— substituted “The micro-purchase threshold for the Department of Defense is $10,000” for “Notwithstanding subsection (a) of , the micro-purchase threshold for the Department of Defense for purposes of such section is $5,000”.Pub.
